brief.definition: Photospheric C/O ratio above or below unity determines whether carbide/carbon dust or silicate/oxide dust and their parent molecules dominate.

brief.name: Circumstellar Dust Nucleation
brief.definition: Gas-to-solid condensation sequences that form the first refractory seed grains in cooling stellar ejecta.

brief.name: Supernova and Nova Ejecta Chemistry
brief.definition: Rapid, shock-processed nucleosynthetic ejecta that seed the interstellar medium with molecules and presolar grain populations.

blocks.name: Grain-Surface and Ice Chemistry
blocks.description: Addresses heterogeneous chemistry occurring on interstellar and protoplanetary dust-grain surfaces and within their accreted ice mantles, where atom and radical mobility enables reactions inaccessible in the gas phase. Explains the formation of molecular hydrogen, water ice, and saturated organic molecules, and the desorption processes that return them to the gas. Central to reconciling observed gas-phase abundances with solid-state astrochemical models.

brief.name: H2 Formation Catalysis
brief.definition: Grain-surface recombination of hydrogen atoms is the dominant formation route for molecular hydrogen in the interstellar medium.

brief.name: Ice Mantle Accretion and Layering
brief.definition: Sequential freeze-out of gas-phase species builds layered polar and apolar ice mantles on cold grain surfaces.

brief.name: Non-Thermal Desorption Mechanisms
brief.definition: UV photodesorption, cosmic-ray spot heating, and chemical desorption release ice-formed molecules back into the gas without thermal sublimation.

brief.name: Laboratory Astrochemistry Simulations
brief.definition: Ultra-high-vacuum ice-analog experiments that measure reaction and desorption rates used to calibrate grain-chemistry models.

blocks.name: Protoplanetary Disk and Planet-Formation Chemistry
blocks.description: Traces how interstellar chemical inheritance is processed, reset, and re-synthesized across the radial and vertical structure of protoplanetary disks as planetesimals and planets assemble. Governs the distribution of volatiles, organics, and isotopic signatures delivered to forming planets and their atmospheres. Bridges molecular-cloud chemistry to solar-system chemical records.

brief.name: Snowlines and Volatile Sequestration
brief.definition: Radial condensation fronts where major volatiles freeze out, setting the solid/gas partitioning of water, CO, and other ices in the disk.

brief.name: Disk Chemical Inheritance vs Reset
brief.definition: Debate over how much protostellar disk material retains parent-cloud molecular composition versus is chemically reprocessed near the young star.

brief.name: Planetesimal and Comet Volatile Budgets
brief.definition: Ice and organic inventories incorporated into comets and planetesimals that constrain the chemical architecture of forming planetary systems.

brief.name: Exoplanet Atmospheric Chemistry
brief.definition: Photochemical and thermochemical equilibrium models of exoplanet atmospheres inferred from transmission and emission spectroscopy.

blocks.name: Cosmochemistry of Meteorites and Presolar Grains
blocks.description: Analyzes the mineralogy, bulk chemistry, and isotopic composition of chondrites, achondrites, and their presolar grain components to reconstruct early solar system conditions and stellar nucleosynthetic sources. Chondrules, calcium-aluminum-rich inclusions, and matrix organics record the earliest solid-forming events before planetary accretion. Foundational to dating and sequencing solar system formation.

brief.name: Chondrites and Chondrules
brief.definition: Primitive stony meteorites containing millimeter-sized silicate spherules formed by rapid melting events in the early solar nebula.

brief.name: Calcium-Aluminum-Rich Inclusions
brief.definition: The oldest dated solar system solids, formed by high-temperature condensation and providing the primary chronological anchor at 4567 Ma.

brief.name: Presolar Grains
brief.definition: Sub-micron mineral grains that condensed in stellar outflows before the Sun formed and survived meteorite parent-body processing intact.

brief.name: Achondrites and Differentiated Bodies
brief.definition: Meteorites from melted, differentiated parent bodies that record igneous processing and core-mantle-crust separation off-Earth.

brief.name: Meteoritic Organic Matter
brief.definition: Insoluble and soluble carbonaceous compounds in carbonaceous chondrites, including amino acids and nucleobases of extraterrestrial origin.

blocks.name: Isotopic Cosmochemistry and Chronology
blocks.description: Uses stable and radiogenic isotope anomalies and decay systematics to fingerprint nucleosynthetic stellar sources, trace chemical fractionation processes, and establish absolute and relative chronologies for solar system solids. Short-lived radionuclide systems date the earliest few million years of solar system history at sub-million-year resolution. Isotopic mass-independent anomalies distinguish nebular from parent-body chemical processes.

brief.name: Radiometric Chronometry
brief.definition: Long-lived decay systems such as Pb-Pb and Ar-Ar isotope dating that anchor absolute ages of meteorites and lunar samples.

brief.name: Short-Lived Radionuclides
brief.definition: Extinct isotopes like 26Al and 60Fe whose decay products give high-resolution relative chronology for early solar system events.

brief.name: Nucleosynthetic Isotope Anomalies
brief.definition: Mass-independent isotope variations among meteorite groups that trace heterogeneous mixing of distinct stellar nucleosynthetic components.

brief.name: Stable Isotope Fractionation
brief.definition: Mass-dependent isotope shifts in O, H, N, and C that record evaporation, condensation, and photochemical self-shielding processes.

blocks.name: Prebiotic Chemistry and Astrobiology
blocks.description: Investigates abiotic synthesis pathways for amino acids, sugars, nucleobases, and other biologically relevant molecules under cosmic and planetary conditions, and the delivery of these compounds to early Earth via comets and meteorites. Connects astrochemical inventories to origin-of-life chemistry and the search for biosignatures elsewhere in the solar system and beyond. A highly active interdisciplinary frontier linking chemistry, planetary science, and biology.

brief.name: Extraterrestrial Delivery Hypothesis
brief.definition: The proposal that comets and carbonaceous meteorites supplied prebiotic organics and volatiles to the early Earth surface.

brief.name: Abiotic Amino Acid and Nucleobase Synthesis
brief.definition: Non-biological reaction pathways, including Strecker synthesis and photochemistry, that produce biomolecule precursors in ices and aqueous fluids.

brief.name: Homochirality Origins
brief.definition: Hypotheses for how a single molecular handedness became dominant in biological building blocks, including circularly polarized light and asymmetric autocatalysis.

brief.name: Biosignature Chemistry
brief.definition: Chemical and isotopic markers proposed to distinguish biological activity from abiotic processes in planetary and exoplanetary environments.

blocks.name: Cometary Chemistry and Solar System Volatile Inventories
blocks.description: Characterizes the coma and nucleus composition of comets and other icy bodies as the least-altered chemical relics of the outer protosolar nebula. Compares volatile and isotopic ratios such as D/H across comets, Earth's oceans, and interstellar ices to test models of water and volatile delivery to the terrestrial planets. Draws on in-situ spacecraft mass spectrometry and remote sensing to complement meteoritic and disk-chemistry constraints.

brief.name: Cometary Coma Composition
brief.definition: Gas and dust released from a sublimating nucleus, measured spectroscopically and in situ to reveal parent volatile abundances.

brief.name: D/H and Isotopic Tracers of Water Origin
brief.definition: Deuterium-to-hydrogen ratios compared across comets, asteroids, and Earth's oceans to constrain the source reservoirs of terrestrial water.

brief.name: In Situ Cometary Mass Spectrometry
brief.definition: Spacecraft rendezvous measurements, such as Rosetta's ROSINA instrument, providing direct molecular and isotopic composition of a comet's coma.

brief.name: Icy Body and Ocean World Chemistry
brief.definition: Chemical characterization of outer solar system ices and subsurface oceans on moons as potential habitable or prebiotic environments.
